english: steṅ (steng) that which is above, the upper part, top, surface, sai steṅ tams-ćád (sa'i steng thams cad) the whole face of the earth Glr.; sén-moi stéṅ-gi sa (sen mo'i steng gi sa) the earth here upon my finger nail Dzl; stéṅ-gi nám-mḱa (steng gi nam mkha') the heavens above Dzl.; stéṅ-gi ṗyogs (steng gi phyogs) the zenith; steṅ-óg (steng 'og) above and below, steṅ-óg-gi ydon (steng 'og gi gdon) demons of the upper and lower regions; stéṅ-na (steng na) adv. and postp.: above, overhead, on high, up-stairs, on the surface, answering to the question where or in what place; stéṅ-du (steng du) adv. and postp. 1. id, answering to the question whither, to what place, but also where or in what place, e.g. to sit on a lotos, to throw down to the ground, to send a thing or a messenger to a person Dzl., frq. 2. above, over, moreover, besides, in addition to, rgás-pai stéṅ-du (rgas pa'i steng du) in addition to my old age Dzl.; byás-pai stéṅ-du (byas pa'i steng du) he made it and besides... Dzl.; bdag ćós-la mi mós-pa méd-pai stéṅ-du bón raṅ-la mos (bdag chos la mi mos pa med pa'i steng du bon rang la mos) I am not only no despiser of religion, but a regular Bon-worshipper Mil.; steṅ-nas (steng nas) down from. — stéṅ-ka (steng ka) (W. *táṅ-ka* (*tang ka*)), also stéṅ-tse (steng tse) a terrace. — stéṅ-ḱaṅ (steng khang) upper story of a house, garret. — *steṅ-dúṅ* (*steng dung*) (?) W. pestle, pounder.

stén-pa (sten pa), pf. and fut bsten (bsten), imp. sten (sten), to keep, to hold; to adhere to, to stick to, to rely or depend on, almost like rtén-pa (rten pa), but c. accus., blá-ma mkás-pa stén-pa (bla ma mkhas pa sten pa) to adhere to a learned Lama; to stick or keep to certain victuals, medicines etc., using them regularly, frq.; even sdug-bsṅál tò (sdug bsngal to) have to taste misfortunes Thgy.; to addict one's self (to virtues or vices), sér-sna to (ser sna to) avarice Stg.; mi stén-pa (mi sten pa) = spáṅ-ba (spang ba) to avoid, shun, abstain from Glr.; Cs. also: yyog stén-pa (g.yog sten pa) to keep a servant in pay.
stém-pa (stem pa), pf. and fut. stems (stems) (= stén-pa (sten pa)?), to hold, to support Mil. nt.; to shut or fasten a door, to secure it by a beam or bar. C.

stér-ba (ster ba), pf. and fut. bster (bster), ccdp. 1. to give B., C., frq.; to bestow, present, grant, concede, allow; with the supine or root of a verb: to let, permit, náṅ-du _gro(r) (nang du 'gro(r)), náṅ-du _óṅ-du (nang du 'ong du) to let enter to grant admission Dzl. — 2. W. in a special sense: to give to eat or to drink, to feed (infants, animals). — 3. to add (in arithmetic) Wdk. — *tér-go* (*ter go*) aid, contribution C.
stes-dbaṅ (stes dbang) Lex., where staṅs-legs (stangs legs) is added for explanation; in Tar. 134, 7 stes-dbaṅ-gís (stes dbang gis) is translated by Schf.: power of fate.

stó-ba (sto ba), most frq. in the col. phrase ćaṅ mi sto (cang mi sto) it does not matter, it makes no difference, it is all the same (also ćaṅ mi rtog (cang mi rtog)); Mil.: śi ruṅ mi stó-ba _dug (shi rung mi sto ba 'dug) it does not matter if they die; śi yaṅ ci stó-ste (shi yang ci sto ste) what does it matter if they die?
(?) stó-ra (sto ra) W., a circle of dancers.

stoṅ (stong) 1. thousand, stoṅ-ṗrág (stong phrag) id., stoṅ-ṗrag-brgyá-pa (stong phrag brgya pa) (the work) containing ten thousand (viz. Sloka) Köpp. II, 272; Burn. I, 462. — stóṅ-dpon (stong dpon) a commander over a thousand; stoṅ-_ḱór-lo (stong 'khor lo) a wheel with a thousand spokes; las stoṅ byed (las stong byed) Med. that is a remedy producing a thousand good effects. — 2. a fine for manslaughter, to be paid in money or goods to the relatives of the person killed; će-ćuṅ-gi stoṅ byéd-pa (che chung gi stong byed pa) Glr., to proportion this fine to the rank of the man killed. — 3. v. stóṅ-pa (stong pa).

stóṅ-pa (stong pa) empty, clear, ḱáb-kyi rtsé-mo tsam yzúgs-pai sa stóṅ-pa (khab kyi rtse mo tsam gzugs pa'i sa stong pa) about so much clear space, as to allow the point of a needle to be stuck in Dzl.; hollow, not charged or loaded (of a gun); not written upon, blank; indifferent, having no distinct or definite quality, e.g. as to taste or smell; rlúṅ-gi raṅ-bźin ni stoṅ mód-kyi (rlung gi rang bzhin ni stong mod kyi) though wind (or air) in itself is without smell Dzl.; waste, deserted, brag-stóṅ (brag stong) a rocky desert, luṅ-stóṅ (lung stong) a desolate
